feed: Create a live intelligence feed covering

cards.title: GPT-5.6 ships — after Washington signs off
cards.caption: GPT-5.6 goes global only after a government green light. The model isn't the story — the precedent is: frontier releases now route through federal review, and 'voluntary' frameworks harden into gates nobody voted on.

cards.title: Meta's new play: sell frontier AI at cost
cards.caption: Zuckerberg's new flagship comes with ultra-low API pricing. Classic Meta: commoditize the layer everyone else monetizes, then earn on what sits above it. Labs selling tokens should be nervous, not impressed.

cards.title: Gemma 4 runs on the Pixel — no cloud, no meter
cards.caption: Google's Gemma 4 runs entirely on the Pixel 10's TPU. Privacy is the ad; the real shift is zero marginal cost — metered-token businesses can't compete with inference that's free after the hardware. The kind of shift a tuned feed catches early.

cards.title: Ticker-promo healthcare AI post
cards.caption: Stock-promotion copy for $AIAI portfolio company; investor-bait, no technical substance, wrong avatar.

cards.title: Grok 4.5 launch retweet
cards.caption: Fan-account RT of a one-line launch headline; no angle, and Grok 4.5 coverage duplicates richer cards elsewhere in the batch.

cards.title: The real AI race isn't at the frontier anymore
cards.caption: Hugging Face's CEO says most production AI already runs on open models. If true, frontier labs' revenue rests on the thin slice of tasks only they can do — and that slice shrinks with every open release. Moat, meet leak.
cards.quote: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier

cards.title: Auto-generated repo roundup
cards.caption: Truncated auto-generated 'fastest-growing projects' list; near-duplicate of cards 09 and 36 in this batch.

cards.title: 'Non asshole funding' HN link
cards.caption: Intriguing phrase, but the card is a link to a screenshot with zero context — nothing to build a take on.

cards.title: Auto-generated repo roundup (dup)
cards.caption: Same Pulrepo auto-roundup as card-07 with reshuffled bullets; near-duplicate.

cards.title: 8B model drives a robot with one RGB camera
cards.caption: Mistral's Robostral navigates from one RGB camera — no lidar, no depth stack. The story is the BOM: when perception becomes software, the expensive part of a robot stops being the sensors. Good week in the embodied-AI lane.

cards.title: 5.24M brain scans learned, zero radiology labels
cards.caption: NeuroVFM learned brain anatomy and pathology from 5.24M raw MRI/CT volumes — no report labels. Labels were the moat in medical AI; self-supervision just turned every hospital archive into a training set. Surfaced in my science lane.

cards.title: July 17: Gemini 3.5 Pro drops, Xi takes the AI stage
cards.caption: Google ships its biggest model the same day Xi opens Shanghai's AI conference in person. That's not coincidence, it's choreography — launch calendars are now instruments of statecraft, and the models are the press release.

cards.title: Stanford's TRACE turns agent failures into curriculum
cards.caption: TRACE diagnoses an agent's repeated failures, then builds RL environments targeting exactly those. That inverts evals: stop benchmarking what agents can do, start compiling what they can't into the training set. Failure logs as data.

cards.title: Perplexity doubles to $8B — for a wrapper?
cards.caption: Perplexity's valuation doubles again while its engine is other labs' models. The market isn't mispricing the tech — it's pricing the interface. Distribution is the asset; the model underneath is just a supplier.

cards.title: More funding won't save your AI startup
cards.caption: In AI, capital converts to compute, and compute buys the same GPUs your competitor rents. Money can't purchase differentiation when the whole stack is rented — which is why the funded and the scrappy keep shipping the same product.
cards.quote: For AI startups, more funding is often not the answer

cards.title: Reply-guy stack musing
cards.caption: One-line reply with a gesture at 'the AI stack'; no specifics, no take to sharpen.

cards.title: July 17 collision (dup)
cards.caption: Same Gemini-launch-meets-Xi story as card-12, weaker presentation; near-duplicate within batch.

cards.title: Can an LLM actually critique a systems paper?
cards.caption: Gauntlet tests whether models can name a paper's core mechanism and surface its buried assumptions — not summarize it. Summarization was solved; structured critique is the real reading test. My arxiv lane flagged it before the summaries did.

cards.title: MIT's robot gym is built by three AI agents
cards.caption: SceneSmith runs a designer, a critic and an orchestrator agent to generate 3D training scenes. Sim-to-real was never bottlenecked on physics — it was scene diversity, and that just became a prompt. Synthetic worlds are the new training data.

cards.title: Karpathy's repo lets models run their own research
cards.caption: Agents run hundreds of experiments, propose code changes, verify, iterate — and a bilevel loop now optimizes the loop itself, 5x faster. Recursive self-improvement didn't arrive with a press event. It arrived as a GitHub repo.

cards.title: July 17 collision (dup 2)
cards.caption: Third copy of the Gemini/Xi same-day story in this batch; card-12 carries it.

cards.title: Bot defense is obsolete — the customers are bots now
cards.caption: Over half of web traffic is automated, and agentic AI makes 'block the bots' the wrong goal. The next decade of security is telling good agents from bad ones — authenticating intent, not humanity. CAPTCHA thinking is dead.

cards.title: Quantum neural nets inherit classical AI's attacks
cards.caption: Input-aware backdoors now work on QNNs — dynamic triggers, not fixed ones. Quantum ML is importing classical security debt onto hardware that barely exists yet. We're porting vulnerabilities faster than we're porting capability.

cards.title: Alibaba just took the top spot in AI voice
cards.caption: Qwen-Audio-3.0 tops the speech arena, ahead of Gemini and Sonic. Voice looks boring until you notice it's the front door for agents and calls — the modality that actually bills. China's labs are quietly winning the commercial surface.

cards.title: Unnamed German startup at $4B
cards.caption: Funding headline without even a company name; nothing to take a position on, and funding cards are over-represented in this batch.

cards.title: The missing agent skill: knowing what it doesn't know
cards.caption: A metacognition survey lands at the right moment — an agent can't ask for help, defer, or escalate without a model of its own uncertainty. Calibration, not raw capability, is what separates a useful agent from a confident liar.

cards.title: Recursive self-improvement, priced by economists
cards.caption: A paper treats RSI as market structure, not sci-fi: if improvement compounds, the first mover's cost curve detaches from the field permanently. The takeoff debate just moved from forums to economics PDFs — which is how you know it's real.

cards.title: Sonnet 5: the mid-tier is where the war is
cards.caption: 63.2% on SWE-bench Pro, cheaper than the flagship, $2/M intro pricing. Flagships are PR; the mid-tier sets what agents cost to run at scale — and intro pricing is a land grab for those workloads. Watch the tier below the headline.

cards.title: Disaster-mapping AI built for hour one, not the benchmark
cards.caption: HASTE assumes what real disasters give you: no matched before/after imagery, no training set from a similar event. Most models optimize for leaderboards; this one, for the first 24 hours. The kind of find a curated feed earns its keep on.

cards.title: Your robot shouldn't need the cloud to hear you
cards.caption: A survey on local ASR in robots asks the quiet question: why does speech go through an API at all? A robot that round-trips to a data center to hear 'stop' is a latency bug with legs. My robotics lane turned this up.

cards.title: LLM verification thread promo
cards.caption: Interesting idea (verification as a scaling axis) buried in a 6/6 promo thread with a gradient-slop tile; the card itself is link-bait.

cards.title: Replit nears $9B
cards.caption: Third bare funding headline in the batch; cards 14 and 15 already carry the funding angle with sharper takes.

cards.title: 'AI FREEDOM IS OVER' roundup
cards.caption: All-caps clickbait roundup ('U.N. Shocked'); pure engagement bait, wrong register for the avatar.

cards.title: Altman's real headline: 54% cheaper agents
cards.caption: The new model is 54% more token-efficient on agentic coding. That's not a capability stat, it's a price cut in disguise — agents multiply token spend, so labs now compete on your COGS. Efficiency is the benchmark that hits the invoice.

cards.title: GPT-5.6 'nearly matches' the leader — that's the story
cards.caption: The frontier gap is measured in weeks now. When every lab lands within noise of the others, capability stops deciding anything — price, latency and distribution do. 'Almost as good' is the most important benchmark result of the year.

cards.title: Auto-generated repo roundup (dup 3)
cards.caption: Fourth Pulrepo auto-roundup in the batch, with visible text-generation glitches ('thweek'); near-duplicate.

cards.title: AI risk math runs on two guessed numbers
cards.caption: The safety model's key inputs — today's catastrophe risk, how fast safety work reduces it — are educated guesses with no data behind them. Doomers and accelerationists are fitting curves to vibes. The confidence on both sides is the tell.
cards.quote: No prior launches and no second chances.

cards.title: ChatGPT Voice launch video
cards.caption: First-party OpenAI promo clip ('turn the sound on for this one'); it's an ad, and the GPT-Live substance lives in better cards outside this batch.

cards.title: Murati's pitch: bake values into the weights
cards.caption: Thinking Machines wants AI distributed, with human values fine-tuned into model weights. Read it twice: if values live at fine-tune time, whoever owns fine-tuning owns the values layer. A decentralization pitch that quietly ends in a market.

cards.title: Grok-written Claude news brief
cards.caption: Auto-generated daily briefing (an AI summarizing another lab's week); meta-amusing but pure regurgitation with no take to extract.
